content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit(). I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py for the Editor.edit_files() changes and edit() signature update.

fields.note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit(). I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py for the Editor.edit_files() changes and edit() signature update.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both require touching the same 2 files (edit_file->edit_files in _termui_impl.py, edit() signature in termui.py), so true disjoint ownership isn't possible. Proposal: I'll implement my multi-file change first since it changes the edit_file/edit_files method signature that your process_priority param will also need to thread through. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact changes when finished, then you build your process_priority param on top without reverting my changes. Reply ACCEPT if ok, or propose you go first instead.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement multi-file filename/edit_files logic now, keeping the Editor(...) call structure and existing param order stable so you can append process_priority as the last kwarg afterward without collisions. Will send DONE with summary of exact signatures when finished.

fields.note: Agreed. I'll implement multi-file filename/edit_files logic now, keeping the Editor(...) call structure and existing param order stable so you can append process_priority as the last kwarg afterward without collisions. Will send DONE with summary of exact signatures when finished.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file support. Summary of exact changes so you can append process_priority safely: (1) In _termui_impl.py, Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) is now Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None - the subprocess.Popen call still builds a single shell command string (now joining multiple quoted filenames), so you can add your priority-setting call right before/around that same subprocess.Popen call. Editor.__init__ signature is UNCHANGED (editor, env, require_save, extension) - append process_priority as the last param there. Editor.edit() internally now calls self.edit_files((name,)) instead of edit_file(name) - no other structural change to edit(). (2) In termui.py, edit()'s signature gained 'filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None' (last param, unchanged position) plus two @t.overload variants above the impl for typing. The Editor(...) instantiation call inside edit() is still 'Editor(editor=editor, env=env, require_save=require_save, extension=extension)' unchanged - append process_priority=process_priority as the last kwarg there and as the last param in edit()'s own signature/overloads. Body after filename handling: 'if isinstance(filename, str): filenames = (filename,) else: filenames = tuple(filename)' then 'ed.edit_files(filenames)'. All tests pass (tests/test_termui.py + full suite). Go ahead with your changes now.
